title: Async Context, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Health, and Incident Evidence · Contract Lens: Structured Log Redaction
summary: The service logs secrets and raw paths, labels metrics by identifiers, loses trace context, reports healthy during saturation, and cannot connect an alert to a user outcome. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts structured log redaction increment in bounded telemetry and health evidence contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Emit structured events with timestamp, severity, stable event name, bounded route, request and trace correlation, outcome, latency, and field-level redaction.
Inject aborts, timeouts, pool exhaustion, ambiguous commits, proxy faults, slow streams, clock shifts, load, and resource leaks with invariant and recovery gates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Structured Log Redaction · Theory Structured Log Redaction
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3371 in a recovery drill following an uncertain transaction commit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to emit bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Emit structured events with timestamp, severity, stable event name, bounded route, request and trace correlation, outcome, latency, and field-level redaction.
